title: Infantryman
description: Serves as the backbone of the Army's ground combat force, engaging and defeating enemy forces in close combat.

title: Indirect Fire Infantryman
description: Operates and maintains mortars, providing indirect fire support to infantry units on the battlefield.

title: Combat Engineer
description: Supports infantry and armor units by constructing bridges, clearing minefields, and demolishing obstacles.

title: Horizontal Construction Engineer
description: Operates heavy construction equipment to build and repair roads, airfields, and other horizontal structures.

title: Carpentry and Masonry Specialist
description: Constructs and repairs wooden and masonry structures including buildings, bunkers, and fortifications.

title: Cannon Crewmember
description: Operates and maintains field artillery cannon systems to provide direct and indirect fire support.

title: Fire Support Specialist
description: Calls for and adjusts indirect fire and close air support, coordinating lethal fires on enemy targets.

title: Patriot Fire Control Enhanced Operator/Maintainer
description: Operates and maintains the Patriot missile system's fire control equipment for air and missile defense.

title: Patriot Launching Station Enhanced Operator/Maintainer
description: Operates and maintains the Patriot missile launching station to protect against aircraft and ballistic missiles.

title: UH-60 Helicopter Repairer
description: Performs maintenance and repairs on UH-60 Black Hawk helicopters to keep them mission-ready.

title: CH-47 Helicopter Repairer
description: Performs maintenance and repairs on CH-47 Chinook helicopters to ensure operational readiness.

title: Unmanned Aircraft Systems Operator
description: Operates and maintains unmanned aerial systems to gather intelligence and support ground operations.

title: Cyber Operations Specialist
description: Conducts offensive and defensive cyber operations to protect Army networks and exploit adversary systems.

title: Cavalry Scout
description: Conducts reconnaissance and surveillance missions, gathering battlefield intelligence ahead of the main force.

title: M1 Armor Crewman
description: Operates and maintains the M1 Abrams main battle tank to engage and destroy enemy forces.

title: Information Technology Specialist
description: Installs, operates, and maintains Army information technology systems, networks, and computer hardware.

title: Nodal Network Systems Operator-Maintainer
description: Operates and maintains tactical nodal network systems that serve as the Army's communication backbone.

title: Satellite Communication Systems Operator-Maintainer
description: Operates and maintains satellite communication systems to provide long-range voice and data connectivity.

title: Signal Support Systems Specialist
description: Provides signal support for command, control, and communications across all echelons of the Army.

title: Military Police
description: Enforces laws and regulations, performs law enforcement and security operations, and supports combat operations.

title: Internment/Resettlement Specialist
description: Oversees the care, custody, and control of enemy prisoners of war, civilian internees, and U.S. military prisoners.

title: Intelligence Analyst
description: Collects, analyzes, and produces intelligence products to support military operations and commanders' decisions.

title: Geospatial Intelligence Imagery Analyst
description: Analyzes imagery and geospatial information from satellites and aircraft to support intelligence operations.

title: Counterintelligence Agent
description: Conducts investigations and operations to identify, assess, and counter foreign intelligence threats to the Army.

title: Human Intelligence Collector
description: Conducts debriefings, interrogations, and elicitation operations to gather intelligence from human sources.

title: Signals Intelligence Analyst
description: Intercepts, analyzes, and exploits enemy electronic signals to produce actionable intelligence.

title: Cryptologic Linguist
description: Applies foreign language skills to intercept, translate, and analyze enemy communications for intelligence purposes.

title: Financial Management Technician
description: Performs accounting, budgeting, and financial management operations to support Army units and installations.

title: Psychological Operations Specialist
description: Plans and conducts psychological operations to influence foreign audiences and support military objectives.

title: Human Resources Specialist
description: Maintains personnel records, processes awards and evaluations, and manages HR functions across Army units.

title: Religious Affairs Specialist
description: Assists Army chaplains in providing religious support, counseling programs, and morale services to soldiers.

title: Practical Nursing Specialist
description: Provides direct patient care under the supervision of physicians and nurses in clinical and field settings.

title: Operating Room Specialist
description: Assists surgeons and nurses during surgical procedures in military hospitals and field medical units.

title: Medical Laboratory Specialist
description: Performs clinical laboratory tests to support diagnosis and treatment of patients in military medical facilities.

title: Radiology Specialist
description: Operates X-ray and other imaging equipment to assist in diagnosing injuries and diseases for Army patients.

title: Combat Medic Specialist
description: Provides emergency medical treatment and primary care to soldiers in both field and clinical environments.

title: Chemical, Biological, Radiological, and Nuclear Specialist
description: Detects, identifies, and decontaminates chemical, biological, radiological, and nuclear hazards on the battlefield.

title: Motor Transport Operator
description: Operates wheeled vehicles to transport personnel, equipment, and supplies in support of military operations.

title: Ammunition Specialist
description: Receives, stores, issues, and maintains conventional ammunition and missiles for Army operations.

title: Wheeled Vehicle Mechanic
description: Maintains, repairs, and troubleshoots wheeled vehicles and associated mechanical systems across the Army fleet.

title: Power-Generation Equipment Repairer
description: Maintains and repairs generators and power generation equipment used to support Army operations worldwide.

title: Allied Trade Specialist
description: Performs metalworking, welding, and machining to fabricate and repair equipment and structural components.

title: Automated Logistical Specialist
description: Manages supply operations using automated systems to receive, store, and issue equipment and supplies.

title: Culinary Specialist
description: Prepares and serves food for Army personnel, managing food service operations in field and garrison environments.

title: Unit Supply Specialist
description: Manages the supply of equipment, ammunition, and repair parts for Army units using automated supply systems.

title: Radio and Communications Security Repairer
description: Maintains and repairs communications security equipment and tactical radio systems used across Army units.
